summaryText: Guest speaker Scott Williams opened the final Sunday of 2025 with a message titled 'Stuck,' based on the story of the paralyzed man at the pool of Bethesda in John 5:1-9. He emphasized that Jesus' question, 'Do you want to be healed?' is a direct challenge to every believer to move from a place of spiritual and emotional paralysis into the wholeness God offers. Pastor Scott identified three primary reasons people remain stuck: staying in the same unhealthy circles, being fixated on what they cannot do, and failing to take up the mat of their past identity and walk in newness of life. He encouraged the congregation to assess their relationships and environments, stop making excuses, and actively participate in their own breakthrough by trusting in Jesus, who sees them beyond their condition. The message concluded with a powerful call to salvation and a commitment to make 2026 a year of decisive change and divine healing.
mainPoints: People stay stuck by remaining in the same unhealthy circles of influence, which can be more toxic than their actual condition.
People stay stuck by focusing on what they cannot do, making excuses instead of answering Jesus' call to be made whole.
People get unstuck when they obey Jesus' command to 'get up, take up your mat, and walk,' actively leaving their old identity and limitations behind.

versesMentioned.text: Some time later, Jesus went up to Jerusalem for one of the Jewish festivals. Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool, which in Aramaic is called Bethesda and which is surrounded by five covered colonnades. Here a great number of disabled people used to lie—the blind, the lame, the paralyzed. One who was there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years. When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in this condition for a long time, he asked him, 'Do you want to get well?' 'Sir,' the invalid replied, 'I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I am trying to get in, someone else goes down ahead of me.' Then Jesus said to him, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.' At once the man was cured; he picked up his mat and walked.
For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.

Monday.scripture: John 5:6 - 'When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in this condition for a long time, he asked him, 'Do you want to get well?''
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel 'stuck' and write down Jesus' question to you: 'Do you want to be healed?'
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that you see me and know my story. Give me the courage to answer 'yes' to your offer of healing today.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 13:20 - 'Walk with the wise and become wise, for a companion of fools suffers harm.'
Tuesday.action: Evaluate one relationship or environment that may be holding you back. Pray for wisdom on whether to create healthy distance.
Tuesday.prayer: God, show me if my circle is helping or hindering my growth. Give me strength to make tough decisions for my spiritual health.

Wednesday.scripture: Philippians 4:13 - 'I can do all this through him who gives me strength.'
Wednesday.action: Replace one 'I can't' statement with 'Through Christ, I can' and take one small step of faith in that area.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I renounce the excuses that keep me paralyzed. I choose to trust in your strength, not my limitations.

Thursday.scripture: John 5:8 - 'Then Jesus said to him, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.''
Thursday.action: Do something tangible that symbolizes leaving an old struggle behind (e.g., delete a toxic contact, discard a negative reminder).
Thursday.prayer: Father, I get up in obedience today. I take up the mat of my past and choose to walk in the new identity you have for me.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!'
Friday.action: Declare your new identity in Christ out loud. Write down 'I am a new creation' and post it where you will see it.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, remind me constantly that I am made new. Help me to live from my future in Christ, not my past failures.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 147:3 - 'He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.'
Saturday.action: Spend time in worship, thanking God for his healing power in your life, even if you don't see the full result yet.
Saturday.prayer: Healer, I put my complete trust in you. I believe you are making me whole, from the inside out. I receive your peace.
